## Callbacks

All callbacks are optional. Define any in `main.lua` and the engine calls them automatically.

```lua
function luna.load()  -- Called once after the script is loaded.
function luna.update( dt : number )  -- Called every frame; dt = elapsed seconds.
function luna.draw()  -- Called every frame for rendering.
function luna.keypressed( key : string, scancode : string, isrepeat : boolean )  -- Key press event.
function luna.keyreleased( key : string, scancode : string )  -- Key release event.
function luna.textinput( text : string )  -- Unicode character typed.
function luna.mousepressed( x : number, y : number, button : number )  -- Mouse button press.
function luna.mousereleased( x : number, y : number, button : number )  -- Mouse button release.
function luna.wheelmoved( x : number, y : number )  -- Mouse wheel scroll.
function luna.gamepadpressed( id : number, button : string )  -- Gamepad button press.
function luna.gamepadreleased( id : number, button : string )  -- Gamepad button release.
function luna.gamepadaxis( id : number, axis : string, value : number )  -- Gamepad axis; value in -1..1.
function luna.joystickadded( id : number )  -- Gamepad connected.
function luna.joystickremoved( id : number )  -- Gamepad disconnected.
function luna.touchpressed( id, x : number, y : number, dx : number, dy : number, pressure : number )  -- Touch begin.
function luna.touchmoved(  id, x : number, y : number, dx : number, dy : number, pressure : number )  -- Touch move.
function luna.touchreleased(id, x : number, y : number, dx : number, dy : number, pressure : number )  -- Touch end.
function luna.focus( focused : boolean )  -- Window focus change.
function luna.visible( visible : boolean )  -- Window show/hide.
function luna.resize( w : number, h : number )  -- Window resized.
function luna.quit()  -- Return true to cancel quit.
function luna.errorhandler( msg : string )  -- Unhandled Lua error.
```

## `luna.graphics` {#graphics}

> Registers the `luna.graphics.*` graphics API.

```lua
luna.graphics.applyTransform()  -- Applies the given Transform object to the current transform stack
luna.graphics.arc()  -- Draws a filled or outlined arc segment centered at (x, y) with the given radius
luna.graphics.captureScreenshot()  -- Captures the current frame as an `ImageData` and passes it to `callback`
luna.graphics.circle()  -- Draws a filled or outlined circle centered at (x, y) with the given radius
luna.graphics.clear()  -- Clears the screen with the current background color
luna.graphics.clearStencil()  -- Resets the stencil mode to the default (keep / always / 0)
luna.graphics.draw()  -- Queues draw commands for all live particles
luna.graphics.drawBatch()  -- Draws all sprites in a SpriteBatch using a single efficient GPU draw call
luna.graphics.drawCanvas()  -- Draws an off-screen canvas to the current render target
luna.graphics.drawEx()  -- Draws any drawable object with a full affine transform
luna.graphics.drawMesh()  -- Draws the given custom Mesh geometry with the current transform and color
luna.graphics.drawNineSlice()  -- Draws a nine-slice image stretched to fill the given rectangle
luna.graphics.drawQuad()  -- Draws a quad region of an image with an affine transform
luna.graphics.ellipse()  -- Draws a filled or outlined ellipse centered at (x, y) with given horizontal and vertical radii
luna.graphics.getBackgroundColor()  -- Returns the current background color
luna.graphics.getBlendMode()  -- Returns the name of the currently active blend mode
luna.graphics.getCameraPosition()  -- Returns the current world-space camera translate offset (cx, cy)
luna.graphics.getCameraRotation()  -- Returns the current camera rotation angle in radians
luna.graphics.getCameraZoom()  -- Returns the current camera zoom (scale) factor applied to the world
luna.graphics.getCanvas()  -- Returns the ID of the currently active render canvas, or nil
luna.graphics.getCanvasSize()  -- Returns the dimensions (w, h) of the current canvas
luna.graphics.getColor()  -- Returns the current drawing color (r, g, b, a)
luna.graphics.getColorMask()  -- Returns the active color channel write mask
luna.graphics.getDefaultFilter()  -- Returns the default texture filter mode
luna.graphics.getDepthMode()  -- Returns the current depth mode string and write-enable flag
luna.graphics.getDimensions()  -- Returns the window dimensions (width, height)
luna.graphics.getFont()  -- Returns the currently active font ID
luna.graphics.getFontAscent()  -- Returns the active font's ascent ÔÇö distance in pixels from baseline to the top of capital letters
luna.graphics.getFontDescent()  -- Returns the active font's descent ÔÇö distance in pixels from the baseline to the bottom of descenders
luna.graphics.getFontHeight()  -- Returns the height of text in the active font
luna.graphics.getFontLineHeight()  -- Returns the line height in pixels of the currently active font
luna.graphics.getFontWidth()  -- Returns the width of text in the active font
luna.graphics.getFontWrap()  -- Returns the wrap mode of the active font
luna.graphics.getHeight()  -- Returns the window height in pixels
luna.graphics.getLineWidth()  -- Returns the current line width in pixels used for 'line' mode drawing
luna.graphics.getMeshTexture()  -- Returns the texture ID currently bound to the given mesh for rendering
luna.graphics.getMeshVertex()  -- Returns position and UV data for a vertex in a Mesh
luna.graphics.getMeshVertexCount()  -- Returns the total number of vertices in a Mesh
luna.graphics.getPointSize()  -- Returns the current point-sprite size
luna.graphics.getScissor()  -- Returns the active scissor rectangle (x, y, w, h), or nil
luna.graphics.getShader()  -- Returns the currently active Shader ID, or nil
luna.graphics.getStackDepth()  -- Returns the current depth of the transform stack
luna.graphics.getStats()  -- Returns a table of renderer statistics (draw calls, triangles, etc.)
luna.graphics.getStencilMode()  -- Returns the current stencil mode as three values: action string, compare string, value
luna.graphics.getWidth()  -- Returns the window width in pixels
luna.graphics.hasShaderUniform()  -- Returns whether the current shader has a uniform with the given name
luna.graphics.intersectScissor()  -- Intersects the current scissor rectangle with the given rectangle
luna.graphics.isWireframe()  -- Returns whether wireframe rendering mode is active
luna.graphics.line()  -- Draws a straight line from (x1, y1) to (x2, y2) using the current color
luna.graphics.newCanvas()  -- Creates an off-screen render canvas and returns its ID
luna.graphics.newFont()  -- Loads a TTF/OTF font file and returns its ID
luna.graphics.newImage()  -- Loads an image file and returns its ID
luna.graphics.newMesh()  -- Creates a custom Mesh from vertex data and returns its ID
luna.graphics.newNineSlice()  -- Creates a nine-slice definition from an image and border insets
luna.graphics.newQuad()  -- Defines a sub-rectangle of a texture (a quad)
luna.graphics.newShader()  -- Compiles a custom WGSL shader program and returns its ID
luna.graphics.newSpriteBatch()  -- Creates a new SpriteBatch for efficiently drawing many sprites sharing one texture
luna.graphics.origin()  -- Resets the transform to the identity (no translation, rotation, or scale)
luna.graphics.points()  -- Draws a list of (x, y) points using the current point size and color
luna.graphics.polygon()  -- Draws a filled or outlined polygon from a flat list of (x, y) vertex coordinates
luna.graphics.polyline()  -- Draws an open multi-segment polyline
luna.graphics.pop()  -- Pops the top transform matrix from the stack
luna.graphics.print()  -- Draws the given text string at (x, y) using the active font and foreground color
luna.graphics.printf()  -- Draws word-wrapped text within a given width
luna.graphics.push()  -- Pushes the current transform matrix onto the transform stack
luna.graphics.rectangle()  -- Draws a filled or outlined rectangle at (x, y) with given width and height
luna.graphics.release()  -- Releases a GPU resource handle and returns its memory to the pool early
luna.graphics.releaseBatch()  -- Releases the sprite batch resource and frees its GPU instance buffer
luna.graphics.releaseCanvas()  -- Releases the canvas render target and frees its GPU framebuffer memory
luna.graphics.releaseFont()  -- Releases the font resource for the given ID and frees its GPU atlas memory
luna.graphics.releaseMesh()  -- Releases the custom mesh resource and frees the GPU vertex buffer
luna.graphics.releaseShader()  -- Releases the compiled shader program and frees its GPU pipeline object
luna.graphics.reset()  -- Resets all graphics state to defaults: transform, color (1,1,1,1), shader, and scissor
luna.graphics.resetCamera()  -- Resets the camera transform to identity ÔÇö no translation, rotation, or zoom
luna.graphics.rotate()  -- Rotates the current transform by the given angle in radians
luna.graphics.scale()  -- Concatenates a scale factor onto the current transform matrix
luna.graphics.sendShader()  -- Sends a named uniform variable value to the currently active shader program
luna.graphics.setBackgroundColor()  -- Sets the RGBA color used to clear the framebuffer at the start of each draw frame
luna.graphics.setBlendMode()  -- Sets the blend equation used when drawing new pixels over the existing framebuffer
luna.graphics.setCamera()  -- Sets the camera transform: position, rotation, and zoom applied to all draw calls
luna.graphics.setCanvas()  -- Redirects all drawing to the given canvas (or screen if nil)
luna.graphics.setColor()  -- Sets the current drawing color for all subsequent draw commands
luna.graphics.setColorMask()  -- Sets which RGBA channels are written to the render target for subsequent draw calls
luna.graphics.setDefaultFilter()  -- Sets the default texture filter mode ('linear' or 'nearest')
luna.graphics.setDepthMode()  -- Sets the depth test comparison mode and optional write flag
luna.graphics.setFont()  -- Sets the active font for subsequent print calls
luna.graphics.setFontLineHeight()  -- Sets the line height multiplier for the active font used in multi-line text rendering
luna.graphics.setLineWidth()  -- Sets the line width for outline drawing
luna.graphics.setMeshDrawMode()  -- Sets the vertex topology mode used when drawing a mesh ('triangles', 'fan', 'strip', 'points')
luna.graphics.setMeshTexture()  -- Binds a texture to the given mesh so it is sampled during rendering
luna.graphics.setMeshVertex()  -- Updates position and UV data for a single vertex in a Mesh
luna.graphics.setMeshVertexMap()  -- Sets an index array defining the vertex drawing order for a custom mesh
luna.graphics.setMeshVertices()  -- Uploads a new flat vertex array to replace the mesh's current geometry
luna.graphics.setPointSize()  -- Sets the diameter in pixels used when drawing point primitives
luna.graphics.setScissor()  -- Restricts drawing to the given rectangle; clears scissor if no args
luna.graphics.setShader()  -- Activates a custom WGSL shader for subsequent draw calls
luna.graphics.setStencilMode()  -- Sets the persistent stencil mode stored in SharedState
luna.graphics.setStencilTest()  -- Configures the stencil test for subsequent draw calls
luna.graphics.setWireframe()  -- Enables or disables wireframe rendering mode
luna.graphics.shear()  -- Applies a shear transform to the current matrix
luna.graphics.spriteBatchAdd()  -- Appends a new sprite to the batch with the given position and transform properties
luna.graphics.spriteBatchClear()  -- Removes all sprites from the batch, resetting its count to zero
luna.graphics.stencil()  -- Draws to the stencil buffer using the given Lua draw function
luna.graphics.translate()  -- Translates (moves) the current transform
luna.graphics.triangle()  -- Draws a filled or outlined triangle with three (x, y) vertex coordinates
```

### `Canvas`

Lua UserData wrapper for an off-screen canvas resource.

```lua
Canvas:getDimensions()  -- Returns the canvas width and height in pixels
Canvas:getHeight() -> integer  -- Returns the canvas height in pixels
Canvas:getWidth() -> integer  -- Returns the canvas width in pixels
```

### `Font`

Lua UserData wrapper for a loaded font resource.

```lua
Font:getAscent() -> number  -- Returns the ascent (distance from baseline to the top of the tallest glyph) in pixels
Font:getDescent() -> number  -- Returns the descent (distance below the baseline for descenders) in pixels
Font:getHeight() -> integer  -- Returns the line height of this font at its loaded size, in pixels
Font:getLineHeight()  -- Returns the line height in pixels used when advancing to the next line of text
Font:getWidth() -> number  -- Measures the rendered width of `text` using this font's current size
Font:setLineHeight()  -- Sets the line height multiplier used when laying out multi-line text
```

### `Image`

Lua UserData wrapper for a loaded texture resource.

```lua
Image:getDimensions()  -- Returns image width and height in pixels
Image:getFilter()  -- Returns the current min and mag texture filters
Image:getHeight() -> integer  -- Returns the image height in pixels
Image:getWidth() -> integer  -- Returns the image width in pixels
Image:getWrap()  -- Returns the current texture wrap mode for the horizontal and vertical axes
```

### `NineSlice`

Lua UserData wrapper for a nine-slice (9-patch) image definition.

```lua
NineSlice:draw()  -- Draws to the current render target
NineSlice:getInsets()  -- Returns the insets
NineSlice:getTextureSize()  -- Returns the texture size
```

### `SpriteBatch`

Lua UserData wrapper for a sprite batch resource.

```lua
SpriteBatch:clear()  -- Removes all sprites from this batch and resets the sprite count to zero
SpriteBatch:getBufferSize()  -- Returns the maximum number of sprites this batch was allocated to hold
SpriteBatch:getCount()  -- Returns the number of sprites currently added to this batch
```

## `luna.window` {#window}

> Window Api implementation for the `lua_api` subsystem.
>
> This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation
> details for window api-related operations and data management.
> Primary functions: `register()`.

```lua
luna.window.close()  -- Requests the application to close the window
luna.window.focus()  -- Requests the window manager to bring the window to the foreground
luna.window.fromPixels( value : number ) -> any  -- Converts physical pixels to device-independent coordinates
luna.window.getDPIScale() -> any  -- Returns the DPI scaling factor for the window
luna.window.getDesktopDimensions() -> any  -- Returns the desktop resolution (width, height) for the given display
luna.window.getDimensions() -> any  -- Returns the window dimensions (width, height)
luna.window.getDisplayCount() -> any  -- Returns the number of connected displays
luna.window.getDisplayName( display : integer? ) -> any  -- Returns the name of the given display
luna.window.getDisplayOrientation() -> string  -- Returns the current display orientation as a string
luna.window.getFullscreen() -> any  -- Returns the current fullscreen type string, or nil if windowed
luna.window.getFullscreenModes() -> any  -- Returns all available fullscreen video modes
luna.window.getGameHeight() -> number  -- Returns the game's logical height in virtual pixels
luna.window.getGameWidth() -> number  -- Returns the game's logical width in virtual pixels
luna.window.getHeight() -> any  -- Returns the window height in pixels
luna.window.getMode() -> any  -- Returns the current window mode settings as a table
luna.window.getNativeDPIScale() -> number  -- Returns the display's native DPI scale factor
luna.window.getPixelDimensions() -> any  -- Returns the window dimensions in physical pixels (for HiDPI)
luna.window.getPosition() -> any  -- Returns the window position (x, y) on the desktop
luna.window.getSafeArea() -> number  -- Returns the safe display area in logical pixels as `x, y, w, h`
luna.window.getScaleInfo() -> table  -- Returns the current viewport scale and offset information as a table
luna.window.getScaleMode() -> string  -- Returns the current viewport scale mode string
luna.window.getSystemTheme() -> string  -- Returns the operating-system color theme preference
luna.window.getTitle() -> any  -- Returns the current text displayed in the operating-system window title bar
luna.window.getVSync()  -- Returns the current VSync mode value
luna.window.getWidth() -> any  -- Returns the window width in pixels
luna.window.hasFocus()  -- Returns whether the window has keyboard input focus
luna.window.hasMouseFocus()  -- Returns whether the mouse cursor is inside the window
luna.window.isHighDPIAllowed() -> boolean  -- Returns whether high-DPI rendering is allowed for this window
luna.window.isMaximized()  -- Returns whether the window is maximized
luna.window.isMinimized()  -- Returns whether the window is minimized
luna.window.isOpen() -> boolean  -- Returns whether the window has been created and is not yet closed
luna.window.isVisible()  -- Returns whether the window is currently visible
luna.window.maximize()  -- Maximizes the window so it fills all available desktop space
luna.window.minimize()  -- Minimizes the window to the OS taskbar or dock
luna.window.requestAttention()  -- Flashes the window in the taskbar to request user attention
luna.window.restore()  -- Restores the window from maximized or minimized state
luna.window.setFullscreen( enabled : boolean, fstype : string? )  -- Enables or disables fullscreen mode
luna.window.setIcon( path : string )  -- Sets the window icon from a pixel buffer
luna.window.setMode( w : integer, h : integer, flags : table? )  -- Resizes the window and optionally changes fullscreen mode
luna.window.setPosition( x : integer, y : integer )  -- Moves the window to the given desktop position
luna.window.setScaleMode( mode : string )  -- Sets the viewport scale mode
luna.window.setTitle( title : string )  -- Sets the text displayed in the window's title bar
luna.window.setVSync( mode : integer )  -- Enables or disables vertical synchronization
luna.window.showMessageBox() -> string  -- Shows a platform native message box dialog
luna.window.toPixels( value : number ) -> any  -- Converts a device-independent coordinate to physical pixels
```

## `luna.input` {#input}

> Input Api implementation for the `lua_api` subsystem.
>
> This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation
> details for input api-related operations and data management.
> Primary functions: `register()`.

```lua
luna.input.getAxis( id : integer, axis : integer ) -> any  -- Returns the current value (-1 to 1) of a gamepad analog axis
luna.input.getAxisCount( id : integer ) -> any  -- Returns the total number of analog axes on the gamepad
luna.input.getBackgroundEvents() -> table  -- Returns whether background gamepad events are enabled
luna.input.getButtonCount( id : integer ) -> any  -- Returns the total number of buttons on the gamepad
luna.input.getCount() -> table  -- Returns the number of connected gamepads
luna.input.getCursor() -> any  -- Returns the currently active cursor ID
luna.input.getGUID( id : integer ) -> any  -- Returns the hardware GUID string of the gamepad
luna.input.getGamepadMappingString( guid : string ) -> string?  -- Returns the stored mapping string for the gamepad with the given GUID, or nil
luna.input.getHat( id : integer, hat : integer ) -> any  -- Returns the direction (string) of a hat switch on the gamepad
luna.input.getJoystickCount() -> table  -- Returns the number of tracked gamepad slots
luna.input.getJoysticks() -> table  -- Returns a list of connected gamepad IDs
luna.input.getKeyFromScancode( scancode : string ) -> any  -- Returns the key name for the given hardware scancode
luna.input.getName( id : integer ) -> any  -- Returns the human-readable name of a gamepad
luna.input.getPosition() -> any  -- Returns the current position (x, y)
luna.input.getPosition( id : integer ) -> any  -- Returns the current position (x, y)
luna.input.getPressure( id : integer ) -> any  -- Returns the pressure (0-1) of the touch with the given ID
luna.input.getRelativeMode()  -- Returns whether relative mouse mode is active
luna.input.getScancodeFromKey( key : string ) -> any  -- Returns the hardware scancode for the given key name
luna.input.getSystemCursor()  -- Returns a system cursor object for the named cursor shape
luna.input.getTouchCount()  -- Returns the number of currently active touch points
luna.input.getTouches() -> table  -- Returns a table of active touch points (id, x, y, pressure)
luna.input.getWheelDelta() -> any  -- Returns the mouse scroll wheel delta (dx, dy) since last frame
luna.input.getX() -> any  -- Returns the current mouse X position in window coordinates
luna.input.getY() -> any  -- Returns the current mouse Y position in window coordinates
luna.input.hasKeyRepeat()  -- Returns whether key-repeat is currently enabled
luna.input.hasTextInput() -> any  -- Returns whether text input mode is currently active
luna.input.isConnected( id : integer ) -> any  -- Returns whether the gamepad with the given ID is connected
luna.input.isCursorSupported()  -- Returns whether cursor customisation is supported on this platform
luna.input.isDown( args : MultiValue ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether the given key is currently held down
luna.input.isDown( button : integer ) -> any  -- Returns whether the given key is currently held down
luna.input.isDown( id : integer, button : integer ) -> any  -- Returns whether the given key is currently held down
luna.input.isGamepad( id : integer ) -> any  -- Returns whether the joystick at the given slot is a recognized gamepad
luna.input.isGrabbed()  -- Returns whether the mouse cursor is locked to the window
luna.input.isModifierActive( modifier : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether the named modifier key (shift/ctrl/alt/meta/super) is currently held
luna.input.isScancodeDown( scancode : string ) -> any  -- Returns whether the key with the given scancode is held
luna.input.isVibrationSupported( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether the gamepad supports haptic vibration
luna.input.isVisible()  -- Returns whether the mouse cursor is currently visible
luna.input.loadGamepadMappings( path : string ) -> integer  -- Loads SDL2 GameControllerDB-format mappings from a file
luna.input.newCursor()  -- Creates a custom mouse cursor from RGBA pixel data
luna.input.saveGamepadMappings( path : string )  -- Saves all stored gamepad mappings to a plain-text file, one entry per line
luna.input.setBackgroundEvents( enable : boolean )  -- Enable or disable receiving gamepad events when the window is not focused
luna.input.setCursor( cursor : any )  -- Sets the currently visible mouse cursor to the given hardware cursor handle
luna.input.setGamepadMapping( guid : string, mapping : string )  -- Stores or replaces the SDL2 GameControllerDB mapping string for the given GUID
luna.input.setGrabbed( grabbed : boolean )  -- Locks or unlocks the mouse cursor to the window
luna.input.setKeyRepeat( enabled : boolean )  -- Enables or disables key-repeat events
luna.input.setPosition( x : number, y : number )  -- Moves the mouse cursor to the given window-space position
luna.input.setRelativeMode( relative : boolean )  -- Enables or disables raw relative mouse motion mode
luna.input.setTextInput( enabled : boolean )  -- Enables or disables Unicode text input mode
luna.input.setVibration( args : MultiValue ) -> boolean  -- Triggers haptic rumble with the given left/right motor strengths
luna.input.setVisible( visible : boolean )  -- Shows or hides the operating-system mouse cursor over the game window
```

## `luna.timer` {#timer}

> Timer Api implementation for the `lua_api` subsystem.
>
> This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation
> details for timer api-related operations and data management.
> Primary functions: `register()`.

```lua
luna.timer.getAverageDelta() -> any  -- Returns the rolling-average frame delta time in seconds
luna.timer.getDelta() -> any  -- Returns the delta time (seconds) for the current frame
luna.timer.getFPS() -> any  -- Returns the measured frames-per-second for the current frame
luna.timer.getMicroTime()  -- Returns the high-resolution monotonic timer value in microseconds
luna.timer.getTime() -> any  -- Returns the total elapsed time in seconds since engine start
luna.timer.newScheduler()  -- Creates an independent timer scheduler for managing a set of callbacks
luna.timer.sleep( seconds : number )  -- Suspends execution for the given number of seconds
luna.timer.step() -> any  -- Advances the timer by one step (called automatically each frame)
```

### `mlua`

```lua
mlua:after( delay : number, func : function ) -> any  -- Calls a Lua function after the given delay in seconds
mlua:cancel( id : integer ) -> any  -- Cancels a scheduled timer callback
mlua:cancelAll() -> any  -- Cancels and removes every pending timer entry from this scheduler
mlua:cancelNamed( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Cancels a specific named timer that was scheduled on this scheduler
mlua:getCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of active timer entries currently in the scheduler
mlua:getInterval( id : integer ) -> any  -- Returns the repeat interval of the given timer entry in seconds
mlua:getRemaining( id : integer ) -> any  -- Returns the time remaining before the next invocation of the given timer
mlua:getRepeatCount( id : integer ) -> any  -- Returns how many times the given timer has fired since it was created
mlua:getTimeScale() -> any  -- Returns the current time-scale factor applied to all timers in this scheduler
mlua:isEmpty() -> boolean  -- Returns whether the scheduler has no active timer entries
mlua:isPaused( id : integer ) -> any  -- Returns whether the scheduler is currently paused and not advancing timers
mlua:pause( id : integer ) -> any  -- Pauses the scheduler so no pending callbacks fire until resumed
mlua:resetEvent( id : integer ) -> any  -- Resets the elapsed time of the given timer so it fires again after its full interval
mlua:resume( id : integer ) -> any  -- Resumes a paused scheduler, allowing its callbacks to fire again
mlua:setInterval( id : integer, interval : number ) -> any  -- Changes the repeat interval of an existing timer without recreating it
mlua:setTimeScale( scale : number )  -- Sets a time-scale factor that speeds up or slows down all timers in the scheduler
mlua:update( dt : number ) -> any  -- Advances all pending timers by dt seconds
```

---

## `luna.math` {#math}

```lua
luna.math.abs()  -- Returns the absolute (non-negative) value of x
luna.math.angleBetween()  -- luna.math.angleBetween(x1, y1, x2, y2)
luna.math.atan2()  -- Returns the angle in radians between the positive x-axis and (y, x)
luna.math.bresenham()  -- luna.math.bresenham(x1, y1, x2, y2)
luna.math.castRay2D()  -- luna.math.castRay2D(ox, oy, dx, dy, maxDist, segments)
luna.math.ceil()  -- Returns the smallest integer greater than or equal to x (rounds up)
luna.math.cellularAutomata()  -- luna.math.cellularAutomata(width, height, opts)
luna.math.circleContainsPoint()  -- luna.math.circleContainsPoint(cx, cy, r, px, py)
luna.math.circleIntersectsCircle()  -- luna.math.circleIntersectsCircle(x1, y1, r1, x2, y2, r2)
luna.math.circleIntersectsLine()  -- luna.math.circleIntersectsLine(cx, cy, r, lx1, ly1, lx2, ly2)
luna.math.circleIntersectsSegment()  -- luna.math.circleIntersectsSegment(cx, cy, r, sx1, sy1, sx2, sy2)
luna.math.clamp()  -- Clamps a value between min and max
luna.math.closestPointOnSegment()  -- luna.math.closestPointOnSegment(px, py, x1, y1, x2, y2)
luna.math.convexHull()  -- Computes and returns the convex hull of a set of 2D points as an ordered vertex list
luna.math.cos()  -- Returns the cosine of the given angle in radians
luna.math.delaunayTriangulate()  -- luna.math.delaunayTriangulate(points)
luna.math.distance()  -- Returns the Euclidean distance between two points
luna.math.distanceShade()  -- luna.math.distanceShade(distance, maxDistance)
luna.math.ease()  -- Applies an easing function to t in [0,1]
luna.math.fieldOfView()  -- luna.math.fieldOfView(ox, oy, segments, radius)
luna.math.floodFill()  -- luna.math.floodFill(data, width, height, sx, sy, threshold, mode)
luna.math.floor()  -- Returns the largest integer less than or equal to x (rounds down)
luna.math.gammaToLinear() -> Linear  -- Converts a sRGB gamma-encoded channel value to linear light intensity
luna.math.getRandomSeed()  -- Returns the current seed of the engine global random number generator
luna.math.isConvex()  -- Returns whether a polygon described by points is convex
luna.math.lerp()  -- Linearly interpolates between a and b by t
luna.math.lineIntersect()  -- luna.math.lineIntersect(x1, y1, x2, y2, x3, y3, x4, y4)
luna.math.linearToGamma() -> Gamma  -- Converts a linear-light channel value to sRGB gamma-encoded space
luna.math.max()  -- Returns the largest value from the given list of numbers
luna.math.min()  -- Returns the smallest value from the given list of numbers
luna.math.newBezierCurve()  -- Creates a new Bezier curve from the given control points
luna.math.newGrid()  -- luna.math.newGrid(width, height, defaultCost)
luna.math.newNoiseGenerator()  -- Creates a reusable Perlin/simplex noise generator with a given seed
luna.math.newRandomGenerator()  -- Creates an independent random generator with its own seed
luna.math.newRaycaster2D()  -- luna.math.newRaycaster2D(width, height)
luna.math.newSpatialHash()  -- Creates a spatial hash grid for fast broad-phase proximity and overlap queries
luna.math.newTileWalker()  -- luna.math.newTileWalker(x, y, facing)
luna.math.newTransform()  -- Creates a new affine Transform object
luna.math.newTween()  -- luna.math.newTween(duration, easing)
luna.math.newVec2()  -- Creates a new 2-component vector object with x and y fields
luna.math.noise()  -- Returns a smooth noise value (1D, 2D, or 3D)
luna.math.normalize()  -- Normalizes an angle in radians into the canonical range [-pi, pi]
luna.math.perlinNoisePeriodic()  -- luna.math.perlinNoisePeriodic(x, y, px, py)
luna.math.pointInPolygon()  -- luna.math.pointInPolygon(vertices, px, py)
luna.math.poissonDisk()  -- luna.math.poissonDisk(width, height, minDist, maxAttempts, seed)
luna.math.polygonArea()  -- Returns the signed area of a simple polygon described by its vertex list
luna.math.polygonCentroid()  -- luna.math.polygonCentroid(vertices)
luna.math.projectColumn()  -- luna.math.projectColumn(distance, fov, screenHeight)
luna.math.random()  -- Returns a pseudo-random number. No args: [0,1). One arg max: [1,max]. Two args min,max: [min,max]
luna.math.randomNormal()  -- Returns a normally distributed random number
luna.math.segmentIntersectsSegment()  -- luna.math.segmentIntersectsSegment(x1, y1, x2, y2, x3, y3, x4, y4)
luna.math.setRandomSeed()  -- Seeds the engine global random number generator with the given integer
luna.math.simplexNoise()  -- Standalone simplex noise: returns a value in approximately `[-1, 1]` for 1–3 coordinates
luna.math.sin()  -- Returns the sine of the given angle in radians
luna.math.sqrt()  -- Returns the positive square root of x
luna.math.tan()  -- Returns the tangent of the given angle in radians
luna.math.triangulate()  -- Triangulates a simple polygon and returns index triples
luna.math.voronoiDiagram()  -- luna.math.voronoiDiagram(width, height, points, opts)
```

## `luna.audio` {#audio}

> Audio Api implementation for the `lua_api` subsystem.
>
> This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation
> details for audio api-related operations and data management.
> Key types exported from this module: `LuaSource`, `LuaBus`, `LuaMidiPlayer`.
> Primary functions: `register()`.

```lua
luna.audio.clearFilter( id_val : any )  -- Removes any active filter from the audio source
luna.audio.clearMidiSoundFont()  -- Unloads the active SoundFont so the MIDI player falls back to built-in defaults
luna.audio.clone( id_val : any ) -> any  -- Creates an independent copy of an audio source
luna.audio.fadeIn( id_val : any, dur : number )  -- Fades the audio source in from silence over the given duration
luna.audio.getActiveSourceCount() -> any  -- Returns the number of currently playing audio sources
luna.audio.getDistanceModel() -> any  -- Returns the current distance attenuation model name
luna.audio.getDopplerScale() -> any  -- Returns the current global Doppler scale
luna.audio.getDuration( id_val : any ) -> any  -- Returns the total duration of the audio source in seconds
luna.audio.getFadeIn( id_val : any ) -> any  -- Returns the current fade-in duration
luna.audio.getFreeBufferCount( qsource_id : integer ) -> any  -- Returns the number of free buffer slots remaining in a queueable source
luna.audio.getHighpass( id_val : any ) -> any  -- Returns the current high-pass filter cutoff frequency
luna.audio.getListener() -> any  -- Returns the 3D listener position
luna.audio.getListener2D() -> any  -- Returns the current 2D listener position (x, y)
luna.audio.getLowpass( id_val : any ) -> any  -- Returns the current low-pass filter cutoff frequency
luna.audio.getMasterVolume() -> any  -- Returns the current master volume scale (0.0 - 1.0) applied to all audio output
luna.audio.getMaxSources()  -- Returns the maximum number of simultaneous audio sources
luna.audio.getMeter() -> any  -- Returns the current peak level of the audio source
luna.audio.getOrientation( id_val : any ) -> any  -- Returns the 6-component orientation of an audio source
luna.audio.getPan( id_val : any ) -> any  -- Returns the current stereo panning of the source
luna.audio.getPitch( id_val : any ) -> any  -- Returns the current pitch multiplier of the source
luna.audio.getPlaybackDevice() -> string  -- Returns the name of the currently active audio output device
luna.audio.getPlaybackDevices() -> table  -- Returns a table containing the names of all available audio output devices
luna.audio.getPosition( id_val : any ) -> any  -- Returns the 3D position of an audio source
luna.audio.getSourceBus( id_val : any ) -> any  -- Returns the bus name the source is assigned to
luna.audio.getSourceCount() -> any  -- Returns the number of currently registered audio sources
luna.audio.getSourceType( id_val : any ) -> any  -- Returns the type string ('static' or 'stream') of the source
luna.audio.getVelocity( id_val : any ) -> any  -- Returns the velocity of an audio source
luna.audio.getVolume( id_val : any ) -> any  -- Returns the current volume of the audio source
luna.audio.hasMidiSoundFont() -> any  -- Returns whether a SoundFont is currently loaded for the MIDI synthesizer
luna.audio.isLooping( id_val : any ) -> any  -- Returns whether the audio source is set to loop
luna.audio.isPaused( id_val : any ) -> any  -- Returns whether the audio source is currently paused
luna.audio.isPlaying( id_val : any ) -> any  -- Returns whether the audio source is currently playing
luna.audio.isStopped( id_val : any ) -> any  -- Returns whether the audio source is stopped
luna.audio.newBus( name : string ) -> any  -- Creates a named audio bus for grouping sources
luna.audio.newDecoder( source : string, buffersize : number? ) -> any  -- Creates a streaming audio decoder for chunked PCM reading
luna.audio.newMidiPlayer( path : string? ) -> any  -- Creates a software MIDI synthesizer player
luna.audio.newQueueableSource( sample_rate : integer, bit_depth : integer, channels : integer, buffer_count : integer? ) -> any  -- Creates a queueable audio source that accepts manually-pushed PCM buffers
luna.audio.newSoundData( args : MultiValue )  -- Creates a raw PCM audio buffer for building procedurally generated sound
luna.audio.newSource( args : MultiValue ) -> any  -- Loads an audio file and returns a source handle
luna.audio.pause( id_val : any )  -- Pauses the audio source at its current position
luna.audio.pauseAll()  -- Pauses all currently playing audio sources
luna.audio.play( id_val : any )  -- Plays the audio source from the beginning
luna.audio.playLooping( id_val : any )  -- Plays the audio source in a continuous loop
luna.audio.playQueueable( qsource_id : integer )  -- Starts playback of a queueable source. PCM output is driven by queued buffers
luna.audio.queueSource( qsource_id : integer, sounddata : userdata )  -- Pushes a SoundData buffer into a queueable source
luna.audio.release( id_val : any ) -> boolean  -- Releases the audio source and frees its memory
luna.audio.resume( id_val : any )  -- Resumes playback of a paused audio source from its current position
luna.audio.resumeAll()  -- Resumes playback on every audio source that is currently paused
luna.audio.seek( id_val : any, pos : number )  -- Seeks to the given time position (in seconds) in the source
luna.audio.setDistanceModel( model : string )  -- Sets the distance attenuation model
luna.audio.setDopplerScale( scale : number )  -- Sets the global Doppler effect scale (1.0 = default)
luna.audio.setHighpass( id_val : any, cutoff_hz : integer )  -- Applies a high-pass filter to the audio source
luna.audio.setListener( x : number, y : number, z : number? )  -- Sets the 3D listener position for spatial audio
luna.audio.setListener2D( x : number, y : number )  -- Sets the 2D listener position for spatial audio
luna.audio.setLooping( id_val : any, looping : boolean )  -- Enables or disables looping playback for the source
luna.audio.setLowpass( id_val : any, cutoff_hz : integer )  -- Applies a low-pass filter to the audio source
luna.audio.setMasterVolume( vol : number )  -- Sets the global master volume (0.0 - 1.0)
luna.audio.setMeter( scale : number )  -- Enables or disables peak metering on the audio source
luna.audio.setMidiSoundFont( path : string )  -- Sets the SoundFont (.sf2) file that the MIDI synthesizer uses for instrument samples
luna.audio.setOrientation( id_val : any, fx : number, fy : number, fz : number, ux : number, uy : number, uz : number )  -- Sets the orientation of an audio source
luna.audio.setPan( id_val : any, pan : number )  -- Sets the stereo panning (-1.0 left to 1.0 right) of the source
luna.audio.setPitch( id_val : any, pitch : number )  -- Sets the pitch (playback speed) multiplier of the source
luna.audio.setPlaybackDevice()  -- Selects the audio output device by name
luna.audio.setPosition( id_val : any, x : number, y : number, z : number? )  -- Sets the 3D position of an audio source for spatial panning
luna.audio.setSourceBus( id_val : any, bus_val : any )  -- Assigns the source to a named audio bus
luna.audio.setVelocity( id_val : any, x : number, y : number, z : number? )  -- Sets the velocity of an audio source for Doppler calculation
luna.audio.setVolume( id_val : any, vol : number )  -- Sets the playback volume (0.0 - 1.0) of the source
luna.audio.stop( id_val : any )  -- Stops playback of the audio source
luna.audio.stopAll()  -- Stops all currently playing audio sources
luna.audio.stopQueueable( qsource_id : integer )  -- Stops playback and drains all queued buffers in a queueable source
luna.audio.tell( id_val : any ) -> any  -- Returns the current playback position in seconds
```

### `Bus`

Lua UserData wrapper for an audio bus. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.

```lua
Bus:getName() -> any  -- Returns the name of this audio bus
Bus:getPitch() -> any  -- Returns the pitch multiplier of this bus
Bus:getVolume() -> any  -- Returns the current volume scale of this audio mixer bus
Bus:isPaused() -> any  -- Returns whether this bus is currently paused
Bus:pause()  -- Pauses all audio sources that are currently playing through this bus
Bus:resume()  -- Resumes all paused sources on this bus
Bus:setPitch( pitch : number )  -- Sets the pitch multiplier for all sources on this bus
Bus:setVolume( vol : number )  -- Sets the volume for all sources routed to this bus
```

### `Decoder`

Lua UserData wrapper for a streaming audio `Decoder`.

```lua
Decoder:decode()  -- Decode the next chunk of audio samples
Decoder:getBitDepth() -> u32  -- Return the bit depth
Decoder:getChannelCount() -> u32  -- Return the number of audio channels
Decoder:getDuration() -> f64  -- Return the total duration in seconds
Decoder:getSampleRate() -> u32  -- Return the sample rate in Hz
Decoder:isSeekable() -> boolean  -- Returns whether this decoder supports seeking
Decoder:release()  -- Release the decoder (no-op in the current model)
Decoder:rewind()  -- Rewind to the beginning
Decoder:seek()  -- Seek to a time offset in seconds
Decoder:tell() -> f64  -- Return the current playback position in seconds
```

### `MidiPlayer`

Lua UserData wrapper for the MIDI player.

```lua
MidiPlayer:getBus() -> any  -- Returns the audio bus that this MIDI player's output is routed through
MidiPlayer:getChannelCount() -> any  -- Returns the number of MIDI channels present in the loaded sequence
MidiPlayer:getChannelInstrument( ch : integer ) -> any  -- Returns the General MIDI instrument index for the given MIDI channel
MidiPlayer:getChannelVolume( ch : integer ) -> any  -- Returns the current volume scale applied to the given MIDI channel
MidiPlayer:getDuration() -> any  -- Returns the total duration of the audio source in seconds
MidiPlayer:getFilePath() -> any  -- Returns the file path of the MIDI file currently loaded into the player
MidiPlayer:getNoteCount() -> any  -- Returns the total number of note events in the loaded MIDI sequence
MidiPlayer:getOriginalTempo() -> any  -- Returns the original tempo written in the MIDI file, in beats per minute
MidiPlayer:getSoundFontPath() -> any  -- Returns the file path of the SoundFont (.sf2) currently loaded into the player
MidiPlayer:getTempo() -> any  -- Returns the current playback tempo in beats per minute
MidiPlayer:getTempoScale() -> any  -- Returns the current tempo scale factor applied on top of the original BPM
MidiPlayer:getTicksPerBeat() -> any  -- Returns the ticks-per-beat (PPQ) resolution defined in the MIDI file header
MidiPlayer:getTrackCount() -> any  -- Returns the total number of tracks in the loaded MIDI sequence
MidiPlayer:getTrackName( idx : integer ) -> any  -- Returns the name string of the given MIDI track from the sequence metadata
MidiPlayer:getVolume() -> any  -- Returns the current volume of the audio source
MidiPlayer:isChannelMuted( ch : integer ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether the given MIDI channel is currently muted
MidiPlayer:isLoaded() -> any  -- Returns whether a MIDI file or data string has been successfully loaded
MidiPlayer:isLooping() -> any  -- Returns whether the audio source is set to loop
MidiPlayer:isPaused() -> any  -- Returns whether the audio source is currently paused
MidiPlayer:isPlaying() -> any  -- Returns whether the audio source is currently playing
MidiPlayer:isTrackMuted( idx : integer ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether the given track index is currently muted
MidiPlayer:load( path : string ) -> any  -- Loads a MIDI file from the given path and prepares it for playback
MidiPlayer:loadData() -> any  -- Loads MIDI data from a Lua string directly into the player
MidiPlayer:pause()  -- Pauses the audio source at its current position
MidiPlayer:play()  -- Plays the audio source from the beginning
MidiPlayer:seek( secs : number )  -- Seeks to the given time position (in seconds) in the source
MidiPlayer:setBus( bus_val : any )  -- Routes the MIDI player's synthesizer output through the given audio bus
MidiPlayer:setChannelMuted( ch : integer, muted : boolean )  -- Mutes or unmutes the given MIDI channel for selective playback
MidiPlayer:setChannelVolume( ch : integer, vol : number )  -- Sets the volume scale for the specified MIDI channel
MidiPlayer:setLooping( looping : boolean )  -- Enables or disables looping playback for the source
MidiPlayer:setOnEnd( cb : any )  -- Registers a callback invoked when the MIDI sequence finishes playing
MidiPlayer:setOnNoteOff( cb : any )  -- Registers a callback invoked for each MIDI note-off event during playback
MidiPlayer:setOnNoteOn( cb : any )  -- Registers a callback invoked for each MIDI note-on event during playback
MidiPlayer:setSoundFont( path : string )  -- Loads a SoundFont (.sf2) file into this player for instrument rendering
MidiPlayer:setTempo( bpm : number )  -- Sets the playback tempo in beats per minute
MidiPlayer:setTempoScale( scale : number )  -- Sets a multiplier applied to the original MIDI tempo during playback
MidiPlayer:setTrackMuted( idx : integer, muted : boolean )  -- Mutes or unmutes a specific track by index for selective rendering
MidiPlayer:setVolume( vol : number )  -- Sets the playback volume (0.0 - 1.0) of the source
MidiPlayer:soloChannel( ch : integer )  -- Solos the given MIDI channel so it is the only one producing sound
MidiPlayer:stop()  -- Stops playback of the audio source
MidiPlayer:tell() -> any  -- Returns the current playback position in seconds
MidiPlayer:unsoloAll()  -- Clears the solo flag on every track so all tracks are audible
MidiPlayer:useDefaultSoundFont()  -- Reverts the player to using the built-in default SoundFont for rendering
```

### `Source`

Lua UserData wrapper for an audio source resource.

```lua
Source:clearFilter()  -- Removes any active filter from the audio source
Source:clone() -> any  -- Creates an independent copy of an audio source
Source:fadeIn( duration_secs : number )  -- Fades the audio source in from silence over the given duration
Source:getDuration() -> any  -- Returns the total duration of this audio source in seconds
Source:getFadeIn() -> any  -- Returns the current fade-in duration
Source:getHighpass() -> any  -- Returns the current high-pass filter cutoff frequency
Source:getLowpass() -> any  -- Returns the current low-pass filter cutoff frequency
Source:getPan() -> any  -- Returns the current stereo panning of the source
Source:getPitch() -> any  -- Returns the current pitch multiplier
Source:getType() -> any  -- Returns the type of this audio source: 'static', 'stream', or 'queue'
Source:getVolume() -> any  -- Returns the current volume multiplier
Source:isLooping() -> any  -- Returns `true` if this source is set to loop
Source:isPaused() -> any  -- Returns `true` if playback is currently paused
Source:isPlaying() -> any  -- Returns `true` if this source is currently playing
Source:isStopped() -> any  -- Returns `true` if playback has stopped (either manually or after the audio ended)
Source:pause()  -- Pauses playback. Call `play()` to resume
Source:play()  -- Starts or resumes playback from the current seek position
Source:resume()  -- Resumes playback of this audio source from its current paused position
Source:seek( pos : number )  -- Seeks playback to `offset` seconds from the start
Source:setHighpass( cutoff_hz : integer )  -- Applies a high-pass filter to the audio source
Source:setLooping( looping : boolean )  -- Enables or disables looping. When enabled, the source restarts automatically when it reaches the end
Source:setLowpass( cutoff_hz : integer )  -- Applies a low-pass filter to the audio source
Source:setPan( pan : number )  -- Sets the stereo panning (-1.0 left to 1.0 right) of the source
Source:setPitch( pitch : number )  -- Sets the playback pitch multiplier. `1.0` is normal pitch; `2.0` doubles frequency
Source:setVolume( vol : number )  -- Sets playback volume. `1.0` is full volume; `0.0` is silent
Source:stop()  -- Stops playback and resets the seek position to the beginning
Source:tell() -> any  -- Returns the current playback position in seconds
```

---

## `luna.physics` {#physics}

> Registers the `luna.physics.*` physics API.

```lua
luna.physics.addDistanceJoint()  -- Adds a distance joint that maintains a fixed separation between two body anchor points
luna.physics.addFrictionJoint()  -- Adds a friction joint that resists relative movement and rotation between two bodies
luna.physics.addGearJoint()  -- Adds a gear joint that couples two revolute or prismatic joints so they move in sync
luna.physics.addMotorJoint()  -- Adds a motor joint that drives one body toward a target position relative to another
luna.physics.addMouseJoint()  -- Adds a mouse joint that applies a spring force pulling a body toward a target point
luna.physics.addPrismaticJoint()  -- Adds a prismatic joint that constrains two bodies to slide along a single axis
luna.physics.addPulleyJoint()  -- Adds a pulley joint linking two bodies through a rope of fixed total length
luna.physics.addRevoluteJoint()  -- Adds a revolute joint that constrains two bodies to rotate around a shared anchor point
luna.physics.addRopeJoint()  -- Adds a rope joint that enforces a maximum distance between two body anchor points
luna.physics.addWeldJoint()  -- Adds a weld joint that rigidly fixes the relative position and angle of two bodies
luna.physics.addWheelJoint()  -- Adds a wheel joint combining a revolute motor and a prismatic spring for vehicle suspension
luna.physics.applyAngularImpulse()  -- Applies an instantaneous angular impulse to the body, changing its spin velocity
luna.physics.applyForce()  -- Applies a force vector to a body at its center of mass
luna.physics.applyForceAtPoint()  -- Applies a world-space force at a given point on the body, potentially adding torque
luna.physics.applyImpulse()  -- Applies an instantaneous impulse to a body
luna.physics.applyTorque()  -- Applies a rotational torque to a body
luna.physics.attachShape()  -- Attaches a standalone shape to a body as a new collider fixture
luna.physics.destroyBody()  -- Removes a body from the physics world
luna.physics.destroyJoint()  -- Removes the given joint from the world and frees its resources
luna.physics.destroyWorld()  -- Destroys a physics world and frees all associated resources
luna.physics.getAngularDamping()  -- Returns the angular damping coefficient of the body that slows its rotation over time
luna.physics.getAngularVelocity()  -- Returns the current angular velocity of the body in radians per second
luna.physics.getBodies()  -- Returns a table of all body IDs currently registered in the physics world
luna.physics.getBody()  -- Returns the body object table for the given body ID within the world
luna.physics.getBodyAngle()  -- Returns the current rotation angle (radians) of a physics body
luna.physics.getBodyContacts()  -- Returns all active contact manifolds where this body touches another body
luna.physics.getBodyCount()  -- Returns the number of bodies in the world
luna.physics.getBodyMass()  -- Returns the mass of a physics body
luna.physics.getBodyType()  -- Returns the type string of the given body
luna.physics.getCollisions()  -- Returns all collision pairs currently overlapping in the physics world
luna.physics.getContacts()  -- Returns a table of all currently active collision contact manifolds in the world
luna.physics.getGravity()  -- Returns the global gravity vector (gx, gy)
luna.physics.getGravityScale()  -- Returns the per-body gravity scale factor applied to the world gravity vector
luna.physics.getJointBodies()  -- Returns the two body IDs connected by the given joint
luna.physics.getJointCount()  -- Returns the total number of joints currently active in the physics world
luna.physics.getJointLimits()  -- Returns the lower and upper angular or linear limits set on the joint
luna.physics.getJointMotorSpeed()  -- Returns the target motor speed of a revolute or prismatic joint
luna.physics.getJointType()  -- Returns a string describing the type of the given joint
luna.physics.getJoints()  -- Returns a table of all joint IDs currently active in the physics world
luna.physics.getLinearDamping()  -- Returns the linear damping coefficient that gradually slows the body's movement
luna.physics.getMeter() -> Pixels  -- Returns the current pixels-per-meter scale set on the physics world
luna.physics.isBullet()  -- Returns whether continuous collision detection (CCD) is enabled on the body
luna.physics.isFixedRotation()  -- Returns whether the body's rotation is locked and prevented from changing
luna.physics.isSleepingAllowed()  -- Returns whether the body is allowed to enter a sleep state when inactive
luna.physics.newBody()  -- Creates a rigid body in the given world
luna.physics.newChainBody()  -- Creates a static chain body from a list of connected edge segments
luna.physics.newChainShape() -> Shape  -- Creates a standalone chain shape from a closed/open flag followed by flat vertex coordinates
luna.physics.newCircleBody()  -- Creates a circle-shaped physics body at the given world position
luna.physics.newCircleShape() -> Shape  -- Creates a standalone circle shape
luna.physics.newEdgeBody()  -- Creates a single-segment edge body connecting two points in world space
luna.physics.newEdgeShape() -> Shape  -- Creates a standalone line-segment shape
luna.physics.newPolygonBody()  -- Creates a convex polygon body defined by a list of local-space vertices
luna.physics.newPolygonShape() -> Shape  -- Creates a standalone convex polygon shape from flat vertex coordinates
luna.physics.newRectangleShape() -> Shape  -- Creates a standalone rectangle shape from half-extents
luna.physics.newRegularPolygonBody()  -- Creates a new regular polygon body
luna.physics.newWorld()  -- Creates a new physics simulation world
luna.physics.queryBoundingBox()  -- Returns all bodies whose axis-aligned bounding boxes overlap the given query rectangle
luna.physics.raycast()  -- Casts a ray from the origin in the given direction and returns the first body hit
luna.physics.raycastAll()  -- Casts a ray and returns every body it intersects, sorted by distance
luna.physics.setAngularDamping()  -- Sets the angular damping coefficient that gradually reduces the body's spin
luna.physics.setAngularVelocity()  -- Sets the angular velocity of the body to the given value in radians per second
luna.physics.setBodyAngle()  -- Sets the rotation angle (radians) of a physics body
luna.physics.setBodyMass()  -- Overrides the simulated mass of the body with the given value
luna.physics.setBodyPosition()  -- Sets the position of a physics body
luna.physics.setBodySize()  -- Sets the width and height dimensions of the body's collision shape
luna.physics.setBodyType()  -- Sets the body type: 'dynamic', 'static', or 'kinematic'
luna.physics.setBodyVelocity()  -- Sets the linear velocity of a physics body
luna.physics.setBullet()  -- Enables continuous collision detection (CCD) on a body to prevent tunneling at high speeds
luna.physics.setFixedRotation()  -- Locks or unlocks the body's rotation axis so it cannot spin in the simulation
luna.physics.setGravity()  -- Sets the global gravity vector for the world
luna.physics.setGravityScale()  -- Sets a per-body multiplier applied to the world gravity vector for this body
luna.physics.setJointLimits()  -- Sets the lower and upper angular or linear limits on a constrained joint
luna.physics.setJointLimitsEnabled()  -- Enables or disables the angular/linear limit constraints on a joint
luna.physics.setJointMotorSpeed()  -- Sets the target motor speed for a motorized revolute or prismatic joint
luna.physics.setLinearDamping()  -- Sets the linear damping coefficient that gradually slows the body's movement
luna.physics.setMeter()  -- Sets the pixels-per-meter ratio used to convert physics world units to screen pixels
luna.physics.setMouseJointTarget()  -- Updates the target world-space point that the mouse joint pulls the body toward
luna.physics.setSleepingAllowed()  -- Sets whether the body is allowed to enter a sleep state when it comes to rest
luna.physics.step()  -- Advances the physics simulation by the given timestep in seconds
```

### `Body`

Lua UserData wrapper for a physics body.

```lua
Body:addFixture()  -- Adds a collision fixture to the body using the given shape definition table
Body:applyForce()  -- Applies a continuous force (accumulates until the next physics step) to the body's centre of mass
Body:applyImpulse()  -- Applies an instantaneous impulse directly to the body's centre of mass
Body:destroy()  -- Destroys the body and removes it from its parent physics world immediately
Body:getAngle() -> number  -- Returns the body's current rotation angle in radians
Body:getBodyType()  -- Returns the type string of the given body
Body:getFixtureCount()  -- Returns the number of collision fixtures currently attached to the body
Body:getMass()  -- Returns the total simulated mass of the body in physics units
Body:getPosition()  -- Returns the body's current world-space position
Body:getVelocity()  -- Returns the current linear velocity vector of the body
Body:setAngle()  -- Sets the body's rotation to `angle` radians (bypasses physics)
Body:setPosition()  -- Teleports the body to the given world-space position (bypasses collision detection)
Body:setSize()  -- Resizes the body's primary collision shape to the given width and height
Body:setVelocity()  -- Sets the body's linear velocity to the given (vx, vy) world-space vector
```

### `Shape`

Lua UserData wrapper for a standalone physics shape.

```lua
Shape:destroy()  -- Destroys the shape. No-op — Lua GC handles cleanup automatically
Shape:getBoundingBox()  -- Returns the axis-aligned bounding box of this shape in local coordinates
Shape:getRadius() -> number  -- Returns the radius of a circle shape
Shape:getType() -> string  -- Returns the shape type string: `"circle"`, `"rectangle"`, `"polygon"`, `"edge"`, or `"chain"`
Shape:setDensity()  -- Sets the density for when this shape is attached to a body
Shape:setFriction()  -- Sets the friction coefficient for when this shape is attached to a body
Shape:setRestitution()  -- Sets the restitution (bounciness) for when this shape is attached to a body
Shape:setSensor()  -- Sets whether this shape acts as a sensor (detects overlaps, no forces)
```

### `World`

Lua UserData wrapper for a physics world.

```lua
World:getBodyCount()  -- Returns the number of bodies in the world
World:getCollisions()  -- Returns a table of all currently active collision pairs in this physics world
World:getGravity()  -- Returns the current world gravity vector
World:isSleepingAllowed()  -- Returns whether bodies in this world are allowed to enter a sleep state
World:setCallbacks()  -- Registers collision begin/end callback functions for this physics world
World:setGravity()  -- Sets world gravity. Default is `(0, 9.81)` (downward)
World:step()  -- Advances the physics simulation by `dt` seconds, resolving collisions and integrating forces
```

---

## `luna.filesystem` {#filesystem}

> Filesystem Api implementation for the `lua_api` subsystem.
>
> This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation
> details for filesystem api-related operations and data management.
> Primary functions: `register()`.

```lua
luna.filesystem.append( path : string, data : string )  -- Opens the file in append mode and writes the given string at the end
luna.filesystem.createDirectory( path : string )  -- Creates a directory and any missing parent directories
luna.filesystem.exists( path : string ) -> any  -- Returns whether the given file or directory exists
luna.filesystem.getDirectoryItems( path : string ) -> any  -- Returns a table containing the names of every file and subdirectory in the given path
luna.filesystem.getIdentity() -> any  -- Returns the identity string used to locate the game's sandboxed save directory
luna.filesystem.getInfo( path : string ) -> any  -- Returns a table of metadata (size, modtime, kind) for a path
luna.filesystem.getSaveDirectory() -> any  -- Returns the sandboxed save data directory path
luna.filesystem.getSource() -> any  -- Returns the absolute path of the directory or archive the game was loaded from
luna.filesystem.getUserDirectory() -> any  -- Returns the current user's home directory path
luna.filesystem.getWorkingDirectory()  -- Returns the current working directory path
luna.filesystem.isDirectory( path : string ) -> any  -- Returns whether the given path is a directory
luna.filesystem.isFile( path : string ) -> any  -- Returns whether the given path is a regular file
luna.filesystem.lines( path : string ) -> any  -- Returns an iterator over lines in a text file
luna.filesystem.load()  -- Loads and compiles a Lua file from the VFS, returning it as a callable function
luna.filesystem.mount()  -- Mounts a directory at a virtual path. Returns true on success
luna.filesystem.newFileData()  -- Loads a file from the VFS into a FileData buffer
luna.filesystem.openFile( path : string, mode_str : string ) -> any  -- Opens a file and returns a readable/writable file handle
luna.filesystem.pollAsync( handle_id : integer ) -> any  -- Poll an async load handle. Returns status, data-or-nil
luna.filesystem.read( path : string ) -> any  -- Reads a text file and returns its contents as a string
luna.filesystem.readAsync( path : string ) -> any  -- Start loading a file in the background. Returns a numeric handle
luna.filesystem.remove( path : string )  -- Permanently deletes the file at the given path from the save directory
luna.filesystem.setIdentity( name : string )  -- Sets the identity string that names the game's sandboxed save-data directory
luna.filesystem.unmount()  -- Removes a virtual mount layer
luna.filesystem.write( path : string, data : string )  -- Writes a string to a file, creating it if needed
```

### `FileData`

```lua
FileData:getFilename()  -- Returns the virtual path this data was loaded from
FileData:getSize()  -- Returns the file size in bytes
FileData:getString()  -- Returns the file content as a Lua string
```

### `FileHandle`

```lua
FileHandle:close()  -- Flushes any pending writes and closes the file handle
FileHandle:flush()  -- Flushes all buffered writes to disk without closing the handle
FileHandle:getMode() -> any  -- Returns the access mode the file was opened with
FileHandle:getSize() -> any  -- Returns the size of the open file in bytes
FileHandle:isEOF()  -- Returns whether the read cursor has reached the end of the file
FileHandle:read( count : integer? ) -> any  -- Reads a text file and returns its contents as a string
FileHandle:readLine() -> any  -- Reads the next line of text from the file and returns it as a string
FileHandle:seek( pos : integer ) -> any  -- Seeks the file position to the given byte offset from the start
FileHandle:tell() -> any  -- Returns the current read/write byte offset from the start of the file
FileHandle:write( data : string ) -> any  -- Writes a string to a file, creating it if needed
```

```lua
ParticleSystem:clone()  -- Returns a deep copy of this object

ParticleSystem:getAlphas()  -- Returns the alphas
ParticleSystem:getCount()  -- Returns the count
ParticleSystem:getEmissionShape()  -- Returns the emission shape

ParticleSystem:pause()  -- Pauses playback
ParticleSystem:reset()  -- Resets state to initial values
ParticleSystem:setAlphas()  -- Sets the alphas
ParticleSystem:setEmissionRate()  -- Sets the emission rate
ParticleSystem:setGravity()  -- Sets the gravity
ParticleSystem:setPosition()  -- Sets the position
ParticleSystem:setRelativeMode()  -- Sets the relative mode
ParticleSystem:start()  -- Begins execution
ParticleSystem:stop()  -- Stops playback
ParticleSystem:update()  -- Advances the simulation by `dt` seconds
```

## `luna.event` {#event}

> Event Api implementation for the `lua_api` subsystem.
>
> This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation
> details for event api-related operations and data management.
> Primary functions: `register()`.

```lua
luna.event.clear()  -- Discards all pending events in the queue
luna.event.newSignal() -> any  -- Event
luna.event.poll() -> any  -- Polls and returns the next event from the queue, or nil if empty
luna.event.pump()  -- Syncs OS-level events into the queue. In Luna2D this is a no-op (push model)
luna.event.push( args : MultiValue )  -- Pushes a custom event onto the event queue
luna.event.quit( code : integer? )  -- Pushes a quit event onto the event queue, requesting the engine to stop
luna.event.restart()  -- Requests that the engine restart at the beginning of the next frame
luna.event.wait()  -- Blocks until the next event arrives or the optional timeout elapses
```

### `Signal`

```lua
Signal:clear( name : string ) -> any  -- Removes all callbacks for the named event
Signal:clearAll() -> any  -- Removes all callbacks across all events
Signal:emit( args : MultiValue )  -- Emits the named event, calling all registered callbacks in order
Signal:getCount( name : string ) -> any  -- Returns the callback count for the named event
Signal:getTotalCount() -> any  -- Returns the total callback count across all events
Signal:remove( handle : integer ) -> any  -- Removes a subscription by handle ID
```

---

## `luna.system` {#system}

> System Api implementation for the `lua_api` subsystem.
>
> This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation
> details for system api-related operations and data management.
> Key types exported from this module: `PowerState`.
> Primary functions: `get_processor_count()`, `get_memory_size()`, `open_url()`, `get_preferred_locales()`.

```lua
luna.system.getArch() -> any  -- Returns the CPU architecture string for the current machine
luna.system.getArgs() -> table  -- Returns the command-line arguments as a table
luna.system.getBatchResults( results : table ) -> any  -- Returns the output table from the most recently completed runBatch call
luna.system.getClipboardText() -> any  -- Returns the current contents of the system clipboard
luna.system.getDebugOverlay()  -- Returns whether the debug overlay is currently visible
luna.system.getEnv( name : string ) -> any  -- Returns the value of the named OS environment variable, or nil if not set
luna.system.getInfo() -> any  -- Returns a table of system information including OS name, CPU model, and installed RAM
luna.system.getLastError() -> any  -- Returns the last unhandled error message, or nil
luna.system.getLogLevel()  -- Returns the name of the current minimum log level for runtime messages
luna.system.getMemorySize() -> any  -- Returns the total amount of installed system RAM in megabytes
luna.system.getOS() -> any  -- Returns the host operating system name ('Windows', 'Linux', 'macOS')
luna.system.getPowerInfo() -> any  -- Returns battery state, percentage charged, and estimated time remaining
luna.system.getPreferredLocales() -> any  -- Returns an ordered list of the user's preferred locale strings (e.g. 'en-US')
luna.system.getProcessorCount() -> any  -- Returns the number of logical CPU cores available
luna.system.getVersion() -> any  -- Returns the Luna2D engine version string
luna.system.log( level : string, message : string )  -- Emit a log message from Lua at the specified level
luna.system.openURL( url : string ) -> any  -- Opens a URL in the system's default browser
luna.system.parseArgs( args : table? ) -> any  -- Parses a command-line argument string and returns a structured key/value table
luna.system.runBatch( tasks : table, opts : table? ) -> any  -- Runs a list of shell commands in parallel and returns immediately without blocking
luna.system.setClipboardText( text : string )  -- Replaces the system clipboard contents with the given string
luna.system.setDebugOverlay( enabled : boolean )  -- Shows or hides the FPS/draw-call debug overlay
luna.system.setLogLevel( level : string )  -- Sets the minimum severity level for runtime log messages
```

---

## `luna.thread` {#thread}

> Registers the `luna.thread` namespace.
>
> Provides Lua-level multithreading: create background threads that run
> independent Lua VMs and communicate via thread-safe channels.

```lua
luna.thread.getChannel( name : string ) -> any  -- Get or create a named global channel (singleton, shared across threads)
luna.thread.newChannel() -> any  -- Create an unnamed thread-safe channel for inter-thread communication
luna.thread.newThread( code : string ) -> any  -- Create a new background thread from a Lua code string
```

### `ThreadHandle`

Lua UserData wrapper for a background thread handle.

```lua
ThreadHandle:getError() -> any  -- Returns the error message if the thread terminated with a Lua error, or `nil` if it completed normally
ThreadHandle:isRunning() -> any  -- Returns `true` if the thread is currently executing
ThreadHandle:start( args : MultiValue )  -- Launches the background thread, passing optional arguments to the Lua script via `...`
ThreadHandle:wait()  -- Blocks the calling coroutine until this thread finishes execution
```

---

## `luna.ai` {#ai}

> Registers the `luna.ai.*` game AI toolkit API.
>
> Provides factory functions and UserData wrappers for AI subsystems:
> AIWorld, Agent, Blackboard, StateMachine, BehaviorTree, SteeringManager,
> QLearner, UtilityAI, GOAPPlanner, InfluenceMap,
> Squad, and CommandQueue.

```lua
luna.ai.newAction( callback : function ) -> any  -- New action
luna.ai.newBehaviorTree() -> any  -- New behavior tree
luna.ai.newBlackboard() -> any  -- New blackboard
luna.ai.newCommandQueue() -> any  -- New command queue
luna.ai.newCondition( callback : function ) -> any  -- New condition
luna.ai.newGOAPPlanner() -> any  -- New goap planner
luna.ai.newInfluenceMap( w : integer, h : integer, cs : number ) -> any  -- New influence map
luna.ai.newInverter() -> any  -- New inverter
luna.ai.newParallel( sp : string?, fp : string? ) -> any  -- New parallel
luna.ai.newQLearner( sc : integer, ac : integer ) -> any  -- New q learner
luna.ai.newRepeater( count : integer? ) -> any  -- New repeater
luna.ai.newSelector() -> any  -- New selector
luna.ai.newSequence() -> any  -- New sequence
luna.ai.newSquad( name : string ) -> any  -- New squad
luna.ai.newStateMachine() -> any  -- New state machine
luna.ai.newSteeringManager() -> any  -- New steering manager
luna.ai.newSucceeder() -> any  -- New succeeder
luna.ai.newUtilityAI() -> any  -- New utility ai
luna.ai.newWorld() -> any  -- New world
```

### `AIWorld`

```lua
AIWorld:addAgent( name : string ) -> any  -- Registers a new agent named `name` in this world and returns its handle
AIWorld:getAgent( name : string ) -> any  -- Looks up a registered agent by name
AIWorld:getAgentCount() -> any  -- Returns the number of agents currently registered in this world
AIWorld:getGlobalBlackboard() -> any  -- Returns a snapshot of the shared world-level blackboard
AIWorld:removeAgent( agent : Agent )  -- Removes and destroys the given agent from this world
AIWorld:update( dt : number )  -- Advances all agents in the world by `dt` seconds, integrating velocity into position
```

### `Agent`

```lua
Agent:addTag( tag : string )  -- Adds a string tag to this agent's tag set (idempotent)
Agent:getBlackboard() -> any  -- Returns this agent's private blackboard for reading or writing typed data
Agent:getDecisionModel() -> any  -- Returns the name of the agent's current decision model
Agent:getMaxForce() -> any  -- Returns the maximum steering force cap
Agent:getMaxSpeed() -> any  -- Returns the maximum movement speed cap in world units/second
Agent:getName() -> any  -- Returns the unique name this agent was registered under
Agent:getPosition() -> any  -- Returns the agent's current world-space position
Agent:getPriority() -> any  -- Returns the agent's scheduling priority level
Agent:getVelocity() -> any  -- Returns the agent's current velocity vector
Agent:hasTag( tag : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns `true` if this agent's tag set contains `tag`
Agent:removeTag( tag : string )  -- Removes a string tag from this agent's tag set (no-op if absent)
Agent:setDecisionModel( model : string )  -- Switches the agent's active decision model at runtime. Valid values: `"fsm"`, `"bt"`, `"utility"`, `"goap"`
Agent:setMaxForce( v : number )  -- Sets the maximum steering force that can be applied per frame
Agent:setMaxSpeed( v : number )  -- Sets the maximum movement speed cap in world units/second
Agent:setPosition( x : number, y : number )  -- Teleports the agent to world-space coordinates (`x`, `y`)
Agent:setPriority( p : integer )  -- Sets the scheduling priority; higher-priority agents are processed first during `update`
Agent:setVelocity( x : number, y : number )  -- Sets the agent's velocity vector in world units per second
```

### `BTNode`

```lua
BTNode:addChild( child_ud : BTNode )  -- Adds child to the collection
BTNode:getChildCount() -> any  -- Returns the child count
BTNode:getCount() -> any  -- Returns the count
BTNode:getNodeType() -> string  -- Returns the node type
BTNode:reset()  -- Resets state to initial values
BTNode:setChild( child_ud : BTNode )  -- Sets the child
BTNode:setCount( n : integer )  -- Sets the count
BTNode:setFailurePolicy( policy : string )  -- Sets the failure policy
BTNode:setSuccessPolicy( policy : string )  -- Sets the success policy
```

### `BehaviorTree`


## `luna.data` {#data}

> Registers the `luna.data.*` binary data, compression, hashing, and encoding API.
>
> This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation
> details for data api-related operations and data management.
> Primary functions: `register()`.

```lua
luna.data.compress( format_str : string, raw_data : string, level : integer? ) -> any  -- Compresses data using the given algorithm ('deflate', 'gzip', 'lz4')
luna.data.decode( format_str : string, encoded : string )  -- Decodes binary data from a buffer according to a pack-format string
luna.data.decompress( format_str : string, compressed_data : string ) -> any  -- Decompresses data using the given algorithm
luna.data.encode( format_str : string, raw_data : string ) -> any  -- Encodes Lua values into a binary string using a pack-format string
luna.data.encodeToml( table : table )  -- Encodes a Lua table as a TOML string
luna.data.getPackedSize( format : string ) -> any  -- Returns the total byte count that pack() would produce for the given format and values
luna.data.hash( algo_str : string, raw_data : string ) -> any  -- Returns the cryptographic hash (md5, sha1, sha256) of the input
luna.data.newByteData( value : any )  -- Creates a new mutable byte buffer of the given size
luna.data.newDataView( data : string, offset : integer?, size : integer? ) -> any  -- Creates a read-only windowed view into a byte string
luna.data.pack( format : string ) -> any  -- Packs values into a binary byte string according to a format string
luna.data.parseToml( input : string )  -- Parses a TOML string and returns a Lua table
luna.data.unpack( format : string, data : string, pos : integer? ) -> any  -- Unpacks values from a binary byte string according to a format string
```

### `DataView`

```lua
DataView:getDouble()

DataView:getInt16()

DataView:getInt8()
DataView:getSize()
DataView:getUInt16()

```

## `luna.dialog` {#dialog}

> Lua API bindings for the `luna.dialog.*` dialog sequencer module.
>
> Provides a `Sequencer` UserData type for visual novel-style text
> presentation with typewriter effect, branching choices, timed pauses,
> and inline callbacks.

```lua
luna.dialog.newSequencer() -> any  -- New sequencer
```

### `Sequencer`

```lua
Sequencer:advance()  -- Advances to the next item
Sequencer:choose( index : integer )  -- Choose on this Sequencer
Sequencer:currentSpeaker() -> any  -- Current speaker on this Sequencer
Sequencer:currentText() -> any  -- Current text on this Sequencer
Sequencer:getChoiceLabels() -> table  -- Returns the choice labels
Sequencer:getChoiceText() -> any  -- Returns the choice text
Sequencer:getSpeed() -> any  -- Returns the speed
Sequencer:getState() -> any  -- Returns the state
Sequencer:isActive() -> any  -- Returns `true` if active
Sequencer:isWaitingForChoice() -> any  -- Returns `true` if waiting for choice
Sequencer:load( script : table )  -- Loads state from persistent storage
Sequencer:off( event : string )  -- Removes a previously registered event listener
Sequencer:on( event : string, func : function )  -- Registers an event listener callback
Sequencer:revealedText() -> any  -- Revealed text on this Sequencer
Sequencer:setSpeed( cps : number )  -- Sets the speed
Sequencer:skip()  -- Skip on this Sequencer
Sequencer:start()  -- Begins execution
Sequencer:update( dt : number )  -- Advances the simulation by `dt` seconds
```

## `luna.entity` {#entity}

> Registers the `luna.entity.*` ECS universe API.
>
> Exposes `Universe` as Lua UserData (`LuaUniverse`) with entity lifecycle,
> component operations, querying, systems, tags, layers, and blueprints.

```lua
luna.entity.newUniverse() -> any  -- New universe
```

### `Universe`

```lua
Universe:addSystem( system : table )  -- Adds system to the collection
Universe:addTag( id : integer, tag : string )  -- Attaches a string tag to the entity, enabling fast tag-based group queries
Universe:bitmapTag( id : integer, name : string )  -- Bitmap tag on this Universe
Universe:bitmapUntag( id : integer, name : string )  -- Bitmap untag on this Universe
Universe:clear()  -- Removes all entries
Universe:defineTag( name : string )  -- Define tag on this Universe
Universe:draw()  -- Draws to the current render target
Universe:emit( args : MultiValue )  -- Emits an event
Universe:get( id : integer, name : string )  -- Returns the current value
Universe:getBitmapTagBit( name : string ) -> any  -- Returns the bitmap tag bit
Universe:getBlueprintComponents( name : string )  -- Returns the blueprint components
Universe:getComponents( id : integer )  -- Returns the components
Universe:getEntities() -> any  -- Returns the entities
Universe:getEntitiesByLayer( layer : integer ) -> any  -- Returns the entities by layer
Universe:getEntitiesByTag( tag : string ) -> any  -- Returns the entities by tag
Universe:getEntitiesSorted() -> any  -- Returns the entities sorted
Universe:getEntityCount() -> any  -- Returns the entity count
Universe:getLayer( id : integer ) -> any  -- Returns the layer
Universe:getSystemCount()  -- Returns the system count
Universe:getTags( id : integer ) -> any  -- Returns the tags
Universe:has( id : integer, name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns `true` if the condition is met
Universe:hasBitmapTag( id : integer, name : string ) -> any  -- Returns `true` if bitmap tag
Universe:hasBlueprint( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns `true` if blueprint
Universe:hasTag( id : integer, tag : string ) -> any  -- Returns `true` if the entity carries the given tag
Universe:isAlive( id : integer ) -> any  -- Returns `true` if the entity `id` is currently active in the universe
Universe:kill( id : integer )  -- Destroys the entity with the given `id`, freeing its slot for reuse
Universe:listBlueprints()  -- List blueprints on this Universe
Universe:query( args : MultiValue )  -- Runs a query and returns matching results
Universe:queryBitmapAll( names : table ) -> any  -- Query bitmap all on this Universe
Universe:queryBitmapAny( names : table ) -> any  -- Query bitmap any on this Universe
Universe:queryBitmapTag( name : string ) -> any  -- Query bitmap tag on this Universe
Universe:release()  -- Releases the underlying resource handle
Universe:remove( id : integer, name : string )  -- Removes the entry from the collection
Universe:removeBlueprint( name : string )  -- Removes blueprint from the collection
Universe:removeSystem( system : table )  -- Removes system from the collection
Universe:removeTag( id : integer, tag : string )  -- Removes a string tag from the entity
Universe:setLayer( id : integer, layer : integer )  -- Sets the layer
Universe:spawn() -> any  -- Creates a new entity in this universe and returns its numeric ID
Universe:update( dt : number )  -- Advances the simulation by `dt` seconds
```
